metadata.name: spectraq_vault
metadata.description: SpectraQ non-custodial trading vault — Anchor program

instructions.name: compute_ma_signal_callback
docs: Real Arcium callback. Must live inside this module so that the
`#[arcium_callback]` macro can inject the (private) `validate_callback_ixs`
security check generated by `#[arcium_program]`.

accounts.name: computation_account
docs: program at callback dispatch time.

accounts.name: vault_state
docs: Vault PDA — passed in via `extra_accs` from `request_signal_computation`.

accounts.name: price_update
docs: Verified Pyth `PriceUpdateV2` for the SOL/USD feed. Caller is
responsible for posting a fresh update via `@pythnetwork/pyth-solana-receiver`
(or any compatible client) before calling this instruction. The
handler validates the feed id against `vault.sol_usd_feed_id` and
rejects updates older than `DEFAULT_MAX_AGE_SECONDS`.

accounts.name: price_update
docs: Pyth SOL/USD push account. Used to derive the slippage floor —
`min_amount_out` must be ≥ 95 % of the oracle-derived expected output.

accounts.name: jupiter_program
docs: Jupiter v6 program. Address-pinned to `JUPITER_V6_PROGRAM_ID`.

accounts.name: comp_def_account
docs: the address here because it's being initialized in this instruction.

instructions.name: mock_callback_signal
docs: Hackathon safety net — admin-only manual signal write. Compiled out
of the binary unless the `mock-mpc` feature is enabled.

accounts.name: authority
docs: Either vault.admin or vault.agent. Validated in the handler so the
constraint on the account struct stays tight (one signer).

accounts.name: payer
docs: The agent funds rent for the Arcium signer PDA + computation account.
We require `payer == vault.agent` so only the agent can queue.

errors.name: MathOverflow
errors.msg: Math overflow

errors.name: Unauthorized
errors.msg: Caller not authorized for this instruction

errors.name: ZeroAmount
errors.msg: Amount must be non-zero

errors.name: ZeroShares
errors.msg: Share amount must be non-zero

errors.name: InsufficientShares
errors.msg: User has fewer shares than requested

errors.name: InvalidSignalState
errors.msg: Signal state does not permit this action

errors.name: TradeSizeExceeded
errors.msg: Trade size exceeds MAX_TRADE_SIZE_BPS of source balance

errors.name: PriceOutOfBounds
errors.msg: Provided SOL/USDC price is outside the sanity bounds

errors.name: SignalDirectionMismatch
errors.msg: Trade direction does not match the latest signal

errors.name: AgentEqualsAdmin
errors.msg: agent and admin must be different keys at vault initialization

errors.name: MintMismatch
errors.msg: Token mint passed does not match the one stored in VaultState

errors.name: VaultAccountMismatch
errors.msg: Token vault passed does not match the ATA stored in VaultState

errors.name: BalanceDesync
errors.msg: Vault state cache is out of sync with the on-chain ATA balance

errors.name: ComputationIdMismatch
errors.msg: Provided computation_id does not match pending_computation

errors.name: Unimplemented
errors.msg: This instruction is not implemented yet

errors.name: PriceStale
errors.msg: Pyth price update is older than max_age_seconds

errors.name: InvalidPythFeed
errors.msg: Pyth price account does not match the configured SOL/USD feed id

errors.name: PythReadError
errors.msg: Failed to read Pyth price update (deserialize/verify error)

errors.name: PriceConfidenceTooWide
errors.msg: Pyth price confidence interval exceeds 1% of price

errors.name: InvalidSwapDestination
errors.msg: Jupiter swap destination ATA does not equal the vault's own ATA

errors.name: SlippageExceeded
errors.msg: min_amount_out is below the oracle-derived slippage floor (5%)

errors.name: JupiterCpiFailed
errors.msg: Jupiter v6 CPI failed (route invalid, insufficient liquidity, etc.)

errors.name: InvalidDestinationIndex
errors.msg: destination_ata_index is out of bounds for remaining_accounts

errors.name: InvalidJupiterProgram
errors.msg: Jupiter program account does not match JUPITER_V6_PROGRAM_ID

errors.name: SwapOutputBelowMinimum
errors.msg: Vault did not receive the expected output token amount post-swap

types.name: ClockAccount
docs: An account storing the current network epoch

types.name: ComputationDefinitionAccount
docs: An account representing a [ComputationDefinition] in a MXE.

types.name: ComputationDefinitionMeta
docs: A computation definition for execution in a MXE.

types.name: ComputationSignature
docs: The signature of a computation defined in a [ComputationDefinition].

types.name: ComputeMaSignalOutput
docs: The output of the callback instruction. Provided as a struct with ordered fields
as anchor does not support tuples and tuple structs yet.

types.name: Epoch
docs: The network epoch

types.name: MXEAccount
docs: A MPC Execution Environment.

types.name: MxeStatus
docs: The status of an MXE.

types.name: NodeMetadata
docs: location as [ISO 3166-1 alpha-2](https://www.iso.org/iso-3166-country-codes.html) country code

types.name: NodeRef
docs: A reference to a node in the cluster.
The offset is to derive the Node Account.
The current_total_rewards is the total rewards the node has received so far in the current
epoch.

types.name: Output
docs: An output of a computation.
We currently don't support encrypted outputs yet since encrypted values are passed via
data objects.

types.name: Parameter
docs: A parameter of a computation.
We differentiate between plaintext and encrypted parameters and data objects.
Plaintext parameters are directly provided as their value.
Encrypted parameters are provided as an offchain reference to the data.
Data objects are provided as a reference to the data object account.

fields.name: feed_id
docs: `FeedId` but avoid the type alias because of compatibility issues with Anchor's `idl-build` feature.

fields.name: publish_time
docs: The timestamp of this price update in seconds

fields.name: prev_publish_time
docs: The timestamp of the previous price update. This field is intended to allow users to
identify the single unique price update for any moment in time:
for any time t, the unique update is the one such that prev_publish_time < t <= publish_time.
Note that there may not be such an update while we are migrating to the new message-sending logic,
as some price updates on pythnet may not be sent to other chains (because the message-sending
logic may not have triggered). We can solve this problem by making the message-sending mandatory
(which we can do once publishers have migrated over).
Additionally, this field may be equal to publish_time if the message is sent on a slot where
where the aggregation was unsuccesful. This problem will go away once all publishers have
migrated over to a recent version of pyth-agent.

types.name: PriceUpdateV2
docs: A price update account. This account is used by the Pyth Receiver program to store a verified price update from a Pyth price feed.
It contains:
- `write_authority`: The write authority for this account. This authority can close this account to reclaim rent or update the account to contain a different price update.
- `verification_level`: The [`VerificationLevel`] of this price update. This represents how many Wormhole guardian signatures have been verified for this price update.
- `price_message`: The actual price update.
- `posted_slot`: The slot at which this price update was posted.

types.name: SetUnset
docs: Utility struct to store a value that needs to be set by a certain number of participants (keys
in our case). Once all participants have set the value, the value is considered set and we only
store it once.

fields.name: sol_usd_feed_id
docs: Pyth feed id for SOL/USD (32 bytes, e.g.
`ef0d8b6fda2ceba41da15d4095d1da392a0d2f8ed0c6c7bc0f4cfac8c280b56d`).
Stored once at init; deposit_sol enforces the price account matches it.

fields.name: reserved
docs: 32 bytes were consumed by `sol_usd_feed_id`. Remaining 32 bytes
preserved for forward-compat (e.g. fee config in v2).

types.name: VerificationLevel
docs: Pyth price updates are bridged to all blockchains via Wormhole.
Using the price updates on another chain requires verifying the signatures of the Wormhole guardians.
The usual process is to check the signatures for two thirds of the total number of guardians, but this can be cumbersome on Solana because of the transaction size limits,
so we also allow for partial verification.
This enum represents how much a price update has been verified:
- If `Full`, we have verified the signatures for two thirds of the current guardians.
- If `Partial`, only `num_signatures` guardian signatures have been checked.
# Warning
Using partially verified price updates is dangerous, as it lowers the threshold of guardians that need to collude to produce a malicious price update.

constants.name: JUPITER_V6_PROGRAM_ID
docs: Jupiter v6 program id. Vault's `execute_trade` validates the
`jupiter_program` account passed in matches this constant before
invoke_signed-ing the swap.

constants.name: MAX_SLIPPAGE_BPS
docs: Maximum allowed slippage versus the Pyth-derived expected output, in bps.
Prevents the agent from setting min_amount_out far below the oracle price
(sandwich/MEV defense). 500 bps = 5%.

constants.name: MAX_TRADE_SIZE_BPS
docs: Structural cap on a single trade: at most 30% of the source ATA balance.
